qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: IP whitelist bypass via forged X-Forwarded-For when useXForwardedFor is enabled
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is rooted in whitelist authorization trusting canonical X-Forwarded-For in whitelist/ip.go, while the advisory is rooted in alias forwarded headers such as X_Forwarded_Proto surviving sanitization and being copied into ForwardAuth or snippet auth subrequests.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only final qualifying finding for this session is an IP whitelist bypass caused by whitelist/ip.go trusting attacker-supplied X-Forwarded-For when useXForwardedFor is enabled. The advisory instead describes a different root cause: alias forwarded headers bypassing sanitization and then influencing ForwardAuth or snippet-based auth subrequests.
evidence: The session manifest lists a single final qualifying finding: /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_1e3057f9/ip_whitelist_x_forwarded_for_bypass.

That finding's exploit path is Attacker request with forged X-Forwarded-For -> IPWhiteLister middleware -> whitelist.IP.IsAuthorized -> allowlist match on attacker-controlled header -> protected handler reached.

The advisory summary and details describe pre-authentication bypass through alias forwarded headers such as X_Forwarded_Proto and X_Forwarded_Host reaching ForwardAuth or snippet auth backends after incomplete sanitization and normalization.

The finding does not identify the advisory's vulnerable auth-subrequest code path or the alias-header sanitization failure; it identifies a separate whitelist authorization flaw in a different function and trust boundary.
